item.title: Danske Bank CEO Eyes First Acquisition Since Laundering Crisis
item.description: Danske Bank A/S Chief Executive Officer Carsten Egeriis says it’s time to start thinking about acquisitions, potentially ending a nearly decade-long hiatus from major deals for Denmark’s largest lender.

item.title: ECB Ready to Hike Again in July If Necessary, Nagel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank is prepared to raise interest rates for a second straight meeting next month if the shock from the war in the Middle East requires it, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

item.title: China Asks Big Banks to Curb Interbank Lending to Ease Cash Glut
item.description: China told big state-owned banks to reduce their lending in the interbank market, according to people familiar with the matter, in an effort to prevent borrowing costs from drifting too far below the policy interest rate.

item.title: SpaceX Shares Indicated More Than 35% Higher in Shadow Trading
item.description: Shadow markets are pricing a blockbuster SpaceX stock debut, indicating a pop of at least 35% for Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company.

item.title: Indonesia Tightens Global Banks’ FX Trading to Boost Rupiah
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank has been tightening oversight of Wall Street lenders and local financial institutions as it tries to stem the rupiah’s decline and support Southeast Asia’s largest economy, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: One Part of UK Is Enjoying a Brexit Boost 10 Years After Vote
item.description: As Labour leadership contenders muse about rolling back Brexit, the one UK region that retained unfettered access to the European Union’s market for goods is thriving.

item.title: Australian Private Credit Firm Makes Bet on UK Loan Manager
item.description: Australian real estate private credit firm Qualitas Ltd. is expanding into Europe with the acquisition of a UK-based loan manager, leveraging on growing demand for commercial financing across the region.

item.title: Iran’s Oil Lifeline Faces ‘Biggest Test Yet’ as China Steps Back
item.description: Iranian oil shipments to China, a resilient trade that’s survived years of US sanctions to provide a crucial economic lifeline to Tehran, is coming under huge strain from waning demand and an American blockade.

item.title: Chinese Robot Appliance Maker Dreame Tech Is Said to Consider IPO in Hong Kong
item.description: Dreame Technology, a Chinese maker of robotic vacuum cleaners and other smart home appliances, is considering a Hong Kong initial public offering as soon as next year, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Bad Bunny’s Sold-Out Shows Illuminate Spain’s New Lifeline: Immigrants
item.description: In the past three years, the country has welcomed more than 3 million arrivals, many from Latin America. They’ve helped transform Spain into the fastest-expanding major economy in the euro zone.

item.title: Infineon to Open German Chip Fab as Part of EU Sovereignty Push
item.description: Infineon Technologies AG is preparing to open its largest single investment, a €5 billion ($5.8 billion) semiconductor factory built with the help of European Union subsidies, as the bloc seeks to boost chip production.

item.title: ECB’s Dolenc Says Hike Was Needed as Data Sound Inflation Alarm
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Primoz Dolenc said Thursday’s interest-rate increase was necessary to keep prices in check while officials consider the broader implications of conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Czech Central Bank Chief Sees Stronger Case for June Rate Hike
item.description: Czech central bank Governor Ales Michl said he sees a stronger argument to raise interest rates in June to contain inflationary pressures.

item.title: Britain’s Inflation Fears Can Be Found in Its Pubs and Cafes
item.description: Britons’ enduring appetite for dining out, movies and other indulgences is giving services companies the power to keep raising prices, creating a headache for inflation-fighters at the Bank of England.

item.title: India May Miss Budget Deficit Target for First Time Since Covid
item.description: India is preparing for a wider-than-expected budget deficit this year, according to an official familiar with the matter, putting it on course to miss its target for the first time since the pandemic as the war in Iran drives up energy subsidy costs and adds pressure on government finances.

item.title: Indonesia Says Export Agency Will Focus on Price, Not Trading
item.description: Indonesia’s commodity export agency will focus on monitoring the price of key raw-material exports instead of intervening in trade, according to a senior official, in an apparent effort to ease industry concerns over a radical natural-resource policy introduced last month.

item.title: Corporate Profits Hit Record High Versus India’s Economy Even as Stocks Struggle
item.description: Companies are upbeat about demand and capital spending, ICICI Securities says.

item.title: Copper Rallies From Three-Week Low as Trump Flags Iran Deal
item.description: Copper rebounded from its lowest close since mid-May after President Donald Trump claimed the US was on the cusp of ending the war with Iran that’s threatening the global economy.

item.title: Humanoid Robot Manufacturer EngineAI Is Said to File for Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Chinese robotics startup EngineAI has filed confidentially for a Hong Kong initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, joining a legion of companies in the sector seeking funding.

item.title: Jakarta Deploys Thousands of Police as Students Protest Prabowo
item.description: Indonesian authorities will deploy thousands of police and soldiers ahead of a planned protest by students, who are angry at the rising cost of living and want President Prabowo Subianto to rein in big-ticket spending and revive the economy.

item.title: Lloyds Taps Japan’s Booming Samurai Market With ¥75 Billion Sale
item.description: Lloyds Banking Group Plc became the latest borrower to sell bonds in the booming Samurai market, where issuance has reached the highest level since fiscal year 2015 amid strong demand from yield-seeking investors.

item.title: Alibaba Bids $1.5 Billion for China Grocer in Fight With Meituan
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. is offering $1.5 billion to acquire Chinese grocery delivery firm Pupu, initiating a bidding war as part of a broader campaign to wrest market share from Meituan in online commerce.

item.title: Chinese Region Pushes Coal Chemicals as Energy Security Fix
item.description: One of China’s top coal-producing regions plans to expand use of the feedstock to manufacture chemicals, even as the practice poses a rising threat to the country’s climate goals.

item.title: Korea’s Kospi Surges 8% as Iran Deal Hopes Lift Chip Stocks
item.description: South Korean stocks jumped as risk appetite improved after President Donald Trump said the US was nearing an agreement with Iran to end the war.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as Trump Signals Imminent Peace Deal With Iran
item.description: Gold held its biggest gain since March after President Donald Trump said the US could sign a deal with Iran over the weekend to end the war that’s rattled global markets and stoked inflation.

item.title: MediaTek’s Rally Signals Shift From Laggard to AI Contender
item.description: MediaTek Inc. shares are poised for their best quarter on record, as investors bet a shift into artificial intelligence chips can help it shed the overhang of its struggling older-tech business.

item.title: ChatGPT Pushed Back on LA Fire Suspect’s Vision of Burning City
item.description: The man accused of setting the massive Palisades Fire in Los Angeles repeatedly asked ChatGPT to create images of a city engulfed in flames and floods, and cursed at OpenAI’s chatbot when it told him the scenery he envisioned was too violent, jurors at his trial were told Thursday.

item.title: Peru Holds Key Interest Rate at 4.25% Amid Knife-Edge Vote Count
item.description: Peru kept borrowing costs unchanged after inflation slowed and with the outcome of the presidential runoff still up in the air.

item.title: Japanese Stocks Set to Rise on Optimism Over Middle East War
item.description: Japanese stocks are poised to rise after President Donald Trump signaled the US is close to signing a deal with Iran, boosting investor confidence.

item.title: Locked Out of IPO, Asia Investors Find New Ways to Bet on SpaceX
item.description: Investors across Asia have been largely shut out of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering, which has forced them to find creative ways to make bets on SpaceX’s $75 billion global spectacle.
